questions.question: Draw a circle of radius $3 \text{ cm}$. Take two points $P$ and $Q$ on one of its extended diameters each at a distance of $7 \text{ cm}$ from its centre. Draw tangents to the circle from these two points $P$ and $Q$.
questions.answer: Two pairs of tangents constructed from points P and Q, each pair having equal lengths of $\sqrt{7^2 - 3^2} = \sqrt{40} \approx 6.32 \text{ cm}$.

steps: Draw a circle with center O and radius 3 cm.
Draw a diameter and extend it on both sides to locate P and Q such that OP = OQ = 7 cm.
Bisect OP and OQ to find midpoints M and N.
Using M as center and MO as radius, draw a circle to intersect the original circle at points A and B.
Using N as center and NO as radius, draw a circle to intersect the original circle at points C and D.
Join PA, PB, QC, and QD to get the required tangents.

questions.subtopic: Construction of tangents to a circle

questions.question: Construct a triangle $ABC$ with side $BC = 6 \text{ cm}$, $AB = 5 \text{ cm}$ and $\angle ABC = 60^\circ$. Then construct a triangle whose sides are $\frac{3}{4}$ of the corresponding sides of the triangle $ABC$. Finally, construct a tangent to the circumcircle of triangle $ABC$ at vertex $A$.
questions.answer: A scaled triangle $A'BC'$ and a tangent line touching the circumcircle at A.

steps: Construct triangle ABC with given dimensions.
Draw an acute angle at B and divide the line into 4 segments to construct the similar triangle A'BC'.
Find the circumcenter O by drawing perpendicular bisectors of AB and BC.
Draw the circumcircle with center O passing through A, B, and C.
Construct a line perpendicular to radius OA at point A to get the tangent.
solution.derivation: The tangent at A is perpendicular to the radius OA, hence $\angle OAT = 90^\circ$.

questions.subtopic: Construction of tangents and similar triangles

questions.question: Draw a pair of tangents to a circle of radius $4 \text{ cm}$, which are inclined to each other at an angle of $60^\circ$. Justify the construction.
questions.answer: Tangents constructed such that the angle between them is 60 degrees.

steps: Draw a circle of radius 4 cm with center O.
Draw a radius OA.
Draw a radius OB such that $\angle AOB = 180^\circ - 60^\circ = 120^\circ$.
At point A, construct a line perpendicular to OA.
At point B, construct a line perpendicular to OB.
The point where these two lines intersect is P. $\angle APB$ will be $60^\circ$.
solution.derivation: In quadrilateral OAPB, $\angle OAP = 90^\circ$, $\angle OBP = 90^\circ$, and $\angle AOB = 120^\circ$. Thus $\angle APB = 360^\circ - (90^\circ + 90^\circ + 120^\circ) = 60^\circ$.

questions.question: Draw two concentric circles of radii $3 \text{ cm}$ and $5 \text{ cm}$. Construct a tangent to the smaller circle from a point on the larger circle and measure its length. Verify the measurement by calculation.

steps: Draw two concentric circles with center O and radii 3 cm and 5 cm.
Mark a point P on the circumference of the larger circle.
Join OP (length 5 cm).
Bisect OP at M.
Draw a circle with center M and radius MO, cutting the smaller circle at A and B.
Join PA and PB, which are the required tangents.
solution.derivation: In right $\triangle OAP$, $OA = 3 \text{ cm}$, $OP = 5 \text{ cm}$. By Pythagoras theorem, $PA = \sqrt{OP^2 - OA^2} = \sqrt{5^2 - 3^2} = \sqrt{25 - 9} = \sqrt{16} = 4 \text{ cm}$.
